title: Improving Soil Health Before Planting Season
excerpt: Simple pre-season practices that improve fertility, moisture retention, and long-term crop performance.
author: Hermes Farms Agronomy Team
content: Healthy soil is the foundation of reliable harvests. Before planting, we begin by testing key nutrient indicators so fertilizer plans match actual field conditions instead of assumptions.
We also add mature organic matter to improve structure and water retention. This helps root development and reduces stress during dry spells.
Finally, we prepare fields with minimal disruption where possible. Protecting soil biology and reducing compaction supports stronger crop establishment throughout the season.
highlights: Run soil tests early and map nutrient gaps
Apply compost or organic inputs before first rains
Avoid unnecessary tillage to preserve soil structure

title: How We Maintain Freshness in Produce Distribution
excerpt: From harvest windows to packaging controls, our process keeps produce fresh through delivery.
author: Hermes Farms Operations Team
content: Freshness starts in the field. We schedule harvests around maturity windows and temperature conditions, then move produce quickly to sorting and packing points.
Each batch is graded and packed based on destination. Retail packs and wholesale packs follow different handling and labeling standards to reduce damage and improve shelf life.
During transportation, we prioritize route efficiency and simple handling rules that protect quality from dispatch to delivery.
highlights: Harvest timing is aligned with market delivery windows
Batch grading reduces quality variation for buyers
Packaging standards are tailored by sales channel

title: Organic Farming: What Buyers Should Know
excerpt: A practical guide for households and retailers on what to look for in responsibly grown produce.
author: Hermes Farms Market Desk
content: For many buyers, 'organic' can feel unclear. A better approach is to focus on how produce is grown, handled, and documented from farm to point of sale.
Ask suppliers about soil programs, pest management methods, and post-harvest handling. Responsible producers can explain their process in simple, verifiable terms.
Consistency matters too. Reliable farms provide quality records, clear communication, and dependable supply schedules over time.
highlights: Look for transparent growing and handling practices
Ask for quality and supply consistency evidence
Work with farms that communicate clearly

title: Water Efficiency Strategies for Open Field Farming
excerpt: Practical irrigation and field planning methods that reduce water waste while protecting yield.
author: Hermes Farms Field Team
content: Water planning begins before irrigation starts. Field slope, soil type, and crop stage all influence the best delivery method.
We use targeted watering schedules and simple monitoring routines to avoid over-irrigation. This reduces disease pressure and protects nutrient balance.
Where possible, we combine irrigation improvements with mulching and soil cover to reduce surface evaporation and improve moisture stability.
highlights: Match irrigation method to field and crop stage
Use mulching to preserve soil moisture
